### Input: Translate to Zolai: And he said, Let us take our journey, and let us go, and I will go before thee.
### Response: Tua ciangin Esau in, “Tu-in paikhawm ni. Na mai-ah kei ka pai ding hi,” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And he said unto him, My lord knoweth that the children are tender, and the flocks and herds with young are with me: and if men should overdrive them one day, all the flock will die.
### Response: Ahih hangin Jakob in ama kiangah, “Naupangte a hat lohna ka topa in na thei hi. Tuuhonte le bawngte, a nongek neite ka lungphamawh hi. Ni khat sung kinak hawl lua leh ganhonte khempeuh si ding uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Let my lord, I pray thee, pass over before his servant: and I will lead on softly, according as the cattle that goeth before me and the children be able to endure, until I come unto my lord unto Seir.
### Response: Na nasempa’ mai-ah ka topa na pai inla, Seir-ah ka topa’ kiang kong tun matengin ka mai-a om ganhingte’ thahatna le naupangte’ hatna tawh kituakin damdamin kong makaih ding hi,” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Esau said, Let me now leave with thee some of the folk that are with me. And he said, What needeth it? let me find grace in the sight of my lord.
### Response: Tua ahih ciangin Esau in, “Kei tawh a om mite pawlkhat, nang’ kiangah hong nusia ning,” a ci hi. Ahih hangin amah in, “A kisapna bang om ahi hiam? Ka topa’ muhna-ah kei maipha hong ngahsak in,” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: So Esau returned that day on his way unto Seir.
### Response: Tua ahih ciangin Esau in tua ni-in ama lampi zui-in Seir-ah a ciahkik hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built him an house, and made booths for his cattle: therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
### Response: Ahih hangin Jakob, Sukkoth lamah pai a, ama a’ ding inn le a ganhingte a’ ding hampheng a lam hi. Tua ahih ciangin tua mun a min Sukkoth, kici hi.

### Response: Tua lai-ah amah in biakna tau khat bawl a, El-Elohe-Israel, ci-in min a phuak hi.

### Response: Ni khat, Jakob le Leah’ tanu Dinah tua gam numeite kiangah va hawh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And when Shechem the son of Hamor the Hivite, prince of the country, saw her, he took her, and lay with her, and defiled her.
### Response: Hiv mi Hamor’ tapa Shekhem, tua gam ulianpa in amah a muh ciangin, amah manin luppih a, amah siasak hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And his soul clave unto Dinah the daughter of Jacob, and he loved the damsel, and spake kindly unto the damsel.
### Response: Amah in Jakob’ tanu Dinah hoihsa mahmah ahih manin itin kamkhum takin hopih hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Shechem spake unto his father Hamor, saying, Get me this damsel to wife.
### Response: Tua ahih ciangin Shekhem in a pa Hamor’ kiangah, “Hih numei ka zi dingin hong laksak in,” a ci hi.

### Response: Shekhem’ pa Hamor, Jakob tawh a kimu dingin ama kiangah va pai hi.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Hamor communed with them, saying, The soul of my son Shechem longeth for your daughter: I pray you give her him to wife.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And make ye marriages with us, and give your daughters unto us, and take our daughters unto you.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And the sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father deceitfully, and said, because he had defiled Dinah their sister:

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And the sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz.
### Response: Elifaz’ tapate: Teman, Omar, Zefo, Gatam, le Kenaz ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Timna was concubine to Eliphaz Esau's son; and she bare to Eliphaz Amalek: these were the sons of Adah Esau's wife.
### Response: (Timna pen Esau’ tapa Elifaz’ zineu hi a, amah in Elifaz tawh Amalek nei hi.) Hihte pen Esau’ zi Adah’ tapate ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these are the sons of Reuel; Nahath, and Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah: these were the sons of Bashemath Esau's wife.
### Response: Reuel’ tapate: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, le Mizzah ahi hi. Hihte pen Esau’ zi Basemath’ tapate ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these were the sons of Aholibamah, the daughter of Anah the daughter of Zibeon, Esau's wife: and she bare to Esau Jeush, and Jaalam, and Korah.
### Response: Esau’ zi, Zibeon’ tapa Anah’ tanu Oholibamah tapate: Jeush, Jalam, le Korah ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: These were dukes of the sons of Esau: the sons of Eliphaz the firstborn son of Esau; duke Teman, duke Omar, duke Zepho, duke Kenaz,
### Response: Esau’ suante hih bang ahi hi. Esau’ ta masa Elifaz’ tate: Teman, Omar, Zefo, Kenaz,
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Duke Korah, duke Gatam, and duke Amalek: these are the dukes that came of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; these were the sons of Adah.
### Response: Korah, Gatam, le Amalek ahi hi. Hihte pen Edom gam sungah Elifaz’ suante hi a, Esau’ zi Adah’ sunga piangte ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these are the sons of Reuel Esau's son; duke Nahath, duke Zerah, duke Shammah, duke Mizzah: these are the dukes that came of Reuel in the land of Edom; these are the sons of Bashemath Esau's wife.
### Response: Esau’ tapa Reuel’ tapate: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, le Mizzah hi a, Edom gam sungah a om Reuel’ suante ahi hi. Amaute pen Esau’ zi Basemath’ sung panin a piangte ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these are the sons of Aholibamah Esau's wife; duke Jeush, duke Jaalam, duke Korah: these were the dukes that came of Aholibamah the daughter of Anah, Esau's wife.
### Response: Esau’ zi Oholibamah’ tapate: Jeush, Jalam, le Korah ahi hi. Hihte pen Esau’ zi, Anah’ tanu Oholibamah’ sung panin a piangte ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: These are the sons of Esau, who is Edom, and these are their dukes.
### Response: Hihte pen Esau (Edom) suante hi a, amaute’ sung pan beh lutangte ahi uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, who inhabited the land; Lotan, and Shobal, and Zibeon, and Anah,
### Response: Tua gam a teng Hori mi Seir’ tapate: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Dishon, and Ezer, and Dishan: these are the dukes of the Horites, the children of Seir in the land of Edom.
### Response: Dishon, Ezer, le Dishan hi a, Edom gam sunga Seir’ suante ahi Hori mite’ sung pan beh lutang ahi uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And the children of Lotan were Hori and Hemam; and Lotan's sister was Timna.
### Response: Lotan’ tapate: Hori le Heman ahi hi. Lotan’ sanggamnu Timna ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And the children of Shobal were these; Alvan, and Manahath, and Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
### Response: Shobal’ tapate: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shefo, le Onam ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these are the children of Zibeon; both Aiah, and Anah: this was that Anah that found the mules in the wilderness, as he fed the asses of Zibeon his father.
### Response: Zibeon’ tapate: Ai-ah le Anah ahi hi. Amah pen a pa Zibeon’ late a cin laitakin gamlakah a sa ciktui a mu pa Anah ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And the children of Anah were these; Dishon, and Aholibamah the daughter of Anah.
### Response: Anah’ tate: Dishon le Anah’ tanu Oholibamah ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these are the children of Dishon; Hemdan, and Eshban, and Ithran, and Cheran.
### Response: Dishon’ tapate: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, le Kheran ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: The children of Ezer are these; Bilhan, and Zaavan, and Akan.
### Response: Ezer’ tapate: Bilhan, Zaavan, le Akan ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: The children of Dishan are these; Uz, and Aran.
### Response: Dishan’ tapate: Uz le Aran ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: These are the dukes that came of the Horites; duke Lotan, duke Shobal, duke Zibeon, duke Anah,
### Response: Hori mite’ beh lutangte: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Duke Dishon, duke Ezer, duke Dishan: these are the dukes that came of Hori, among their dukes in the land of Seir.
### Response: Dishon, Ezer, le Dishan ahi hi. Hihte pen Seir gam sungah amau’ beh ciatin Hori mite’ sung pan beh lutang ahi uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these are the kings that reigned in the land of Edom, before there reigned any king over the children of Israel.
### Response: Hihte pen Israel mite tungah kumpi khat beek in a uk ma-in Edom gam sunga kumpite ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom: and the name of his city was Dinhabah.
### Response: Beor’ tapa Bela in Edom gam sung uk a, ama khuapi min Dinhabah ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Bela died, and Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his stead.
### Response: Bela si a, Bozrah khua mi Zerah’ tapa Jobab in ama tangin a uk hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Jobab died, and Husham of the land of Temani reigned in his stead.
### Response: Jobab si a, Teman mite’ gammi Husham in ama tangin a uk hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad, who smote Midian in the field of Moab, reigned in his stead: and the name of his city was Avith.
### Response: Husham si a, Moab gam sungah Midian mite a zo Bedad’ tapa Hadad in ama tangin uk a, ama khuapi min Avith ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his stead.
### Response: Hadad si a, Masrekah khua mi Samlah in ama tangin uk hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Samlah died, and Saul of Rehoboth by the river reigned in his stead.
### Response: Samlah si a, Eufratis gun gei-a Rehoboth khua mi Shaul in ama tangin a uk hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Saul died, and Baal–hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his stead.
### Response: Shaul si a, Akbor’ tapa Baalhanan in ama tangin a uk hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Baal–hanan the son of Achbor died, and Hadar reigned in his stead: and the name of his city was Pau; and his wife's name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezahab.
### Response: Akbor’ tapa Baalhanan si a, Hadar in ama tangin uk a, ama khuapi min Pau ahi hi. Ama zi’ min Mehetabel hi a, Mezahab’ tanu Matred, Matred tanu Mehetabel ahi hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And these are the names of the dukes that came of Esau, according to their families, after their places, by their names; duke Timnah, duke Alvah, duke Jetheth,
### Response: Amau’ innkuan le a tenna, amau’ min tawh kizui-in, Esau’ suan sung pan minamte’ min: Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Duke Aholibamah, duke Elah, duke Pinon,
### Response: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Duke Kenaz, duke Teman, duke Mibzar,
### Response: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Duke Magdiel, duke Iram: these be the dukes of Edom, according to their habitations in the land of their possession: he is Esau the father of the Edomites.
### Response: Magdiel, le Iram ahi hi. Hihte in amau’ neih, amau’ tenna leitang min dingin amau’ min mah vawh pah uh a, Esau’ suan, Edom mi a kicite ahi uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Jacob dwelt in the land wherein his father was a stranger, in the land of Canaan.
### Response: A pa tawlkhat a tenna Kanaan gamah Jakob teng hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: These are the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was feeding the flock with his brethren; and the lad was with the sons of Bilhah, and with the sons of Zilpah, his father's wives: and Joseph brought unto his father their evil report.
### Response: Jakob innkuante’ tangthu hih bang ahi hi. Josef kum sawm le sagih pha ahih manin a sanggamte tawh tuuhon cing hi. A pa’ zite Bilhah le Zilpah’ tapate tawh tangval ahi uh hi. Josef in a pa uh kiangah amaute’ siat gamtatnate a gen hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he was the son of his old age: and he made him a coat of many colours.
### Response: Tu-in Israel in a ta dangte khat peuhpeuh sangin Josef itzaw hi. Bang hang hiam cih leh a teek khitin a neih a tapa ahi hi. Amah in ama a dingin puantualpi bansau khat a bawlsak hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And when his brethren saw that their father loved him more than all his brethren, they hated him, and could not speak peaceably unto him.
### Response: Ahih hangin a pa un a sanggamte khempeuh sangin amah a itzawkna a sanggamte in a muh uh ciangin, amaute in Josef mudah uh a, nuamtakin amah hopih thei lo uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Joseph dreamed a dream, and he told it his brethren: and they hated him yet the more.
### Response: Tu-in Josef in mang khat man a, amah in a sanggamte’ lakah a gen ciangin amaute in amah muhdah semsemna-in nei uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And he said unto them, Hear, I pray you, this dream which I have dreamed:
### Response: Amah in amaute kiangah, “Keima mat hih mang ngai un:
### Input: Translate to Zolai: For, behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and, lo, my sheaf arose, and also stood upright; and, behold, your sheaves stood round about, and made obeisance to my sheaf.
### Response: Eite in lo lai-ah buhlom i hen a, keima buhlom tho-in ding tang hi. Tua kiimah note’ buhlom kikhawm a, keima buhlom mai-ah kun uh hi,” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And his brethren said to him, Shalt thou indeed reign over us? or shalt thou indeed have dominion over us? And they hated him yet the more for his dreams, and for his words.
### Response: A sanggamte in ama kiangah, “Kote’ tungah nang in hong uk ding a, kote’ tungah ukna aana nei ding na hi hiam?” a ci uh hi. Tua ahih ciangin amaute in a mangte le a thugente’ hangin amah mudah semsem uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And he dreamed yet another dream, and told it his brethren, and said, Behold, I have dreamed a dream more; and, behold, the sun and the moon and the eleven stars made obeisance to me.
### Response: Tua ciangin amah in mang dang khat man a, tua mang a sanggamte kiangah genin, “Mang dang khat ka man hi. Ni, kha, le aksi sawm le khatte kei’ mai-ah hong kun uh hi,” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And he told it to his father, and to his brethren: and his father rebuked him, and said unto him, What is this dream that thou hast dreamed? Shall I and thy mother and thy brethren indeed come to bow down ourselves to thee to the earth?
### Response: Ahih hangin amah in a pa le a sanggamte’ kiangah a gen ciangin a pa in amah taii-in, “Nangma mat hih mang a bangci mang ahi hiam? Kei le na nu le na sanggamte, nangma mai leilakah a kun dingin hong pai ding ka hi uh hiam?” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And his brethren envied him; but his father observed the saying.
### Response: A sanggamte in amah haza uh a, ahih hangin a pa in hih thute a ciamteh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And his brethren went to feed their father's flock in Shechem.
### Response: Khatvei a pa uh’ tuuhonte a cing dingin Shekhem gei-ah a sanggamte a pai hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Israel said unto Joseph, Do not thy brethren feed the flock in Shechem? come, and I will send thee unto them. And he said to him, Here am I.
### Response: Israel in Josef kiangah, “Na sanggamte in Shekhem-ah tuuhon cing uh hi lo ahi hiam? Hong pai in, amau’ kiangah hong sawl ning,” a ci hi. Amah in a kiangah, “Hiah om ing,” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And he said to him, Go, I pray thee, see whether it be well with thy brethren, and well with the flocks; and bring me word again. So he sent him out of the vale of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.
### Response: Tua ahih ciangin amah in, “Tu-in va pai inla, na sanggamte le tuuhonte a cidam hiam va thei inla, kei kiangah thu hong zasakkik in,” a ci hi. Tua ahih ciangin amah in Hebron kuam panin Josef sawl a, Shekhem-ah amah va tung hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And a certain man found him, and, behold, he was wandering in the field: and the man asked him, saying, What seekest thou?
### Response: Gamlakah a vak kawikawina mi khat in mu a, “Bang zongzong na hi hiam?” ci-in a dong hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And he said, I seek my brethren: tell me, I pray thee, where they feed their flocks.
### Response: “Ka sanggamte a zong ka hi hi. Amaute in koi lai-ah tuuhon cing uh hiam, hong gen in, hong thum ing,” amah in ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And the man said, They are departed hence; for I heard them say, Let us go to Dothan. And Joseph went after his brethren, and found them in Dothan.
### Response: Mipa in, “Amaute paikhia zo uh hi. Bang hang hiam cih leh, ‘Dothan-ah pai ni,’ a cih uh ka za hi,” a ci hi. Tua ahih ciangin a sanggamte’ nungah Josef pai a, Dothan-ah amaute a mu hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And when they saw him afar off, even before he came near unto them, they conspired against him to slay him.
### Response: Amaute in gamlapi-ah amah na mu uh a, amaute’ nai-ah amah hong tun ma-in amaute in amah thah ding na vaihawm uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And they said one to another, Behold, this dreamer cometh.
### Response: Amaute khatlekhat in, “Hih lai-ah mangman pa hong pai hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Come now therefore, and let us slay him, and cast him into some pit, and we will say, Some evil beast hath devoured him: and we shall see what will become of his dreams.
### Response: Tu-in hong pai un. Amah thatin dum sung khatah lawn ni. Tua ciangin gamsa khat in amah netum khin hi, i ci ding hi. A mangte bang a suak hiam i mu ding hi,” a kici uh hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Reuben heard it, and he delivered him out of their hands; and said, Let us not kill him.
### Response: Ahih hangin Reuben in tua thu a zak ciangin, “Amah that kei ni,” ci-in, amau’ khutsung panin amah honkhia hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Reuben said unto them, Shed no blood, but cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness, and lay no hand upon him; that he might rid him out of their hands, to deliver him to his father again.
### Response: A pa’ kiang a tunpihkik theih nadingin amau’ khutsung pan a hotkhiat theih nadingin Reuben in amaute’ kiangah, “Amah that kei un. Hih gamlak dum sungah amah lawn un. Ahih hangin amah bangmah cih kei un,” a ci hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And it came to pass, when Joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stript Joseph out of his coat, his coat of many colours that was on him;

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And they took him, and cast him into a pit: and the pit was empty, there was no water in it.
### Response: Amaute in Josef la-in dum sung khatah a lawn uh hi. A dum pen a hawm hi a, a sungah tui om lo hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: And they sat down to eat bread: and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and, behold, a company of Ishmaelites came from Gilead with their camels bearing spicery and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: Come, and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him; for he is our brother and our flesh. And his brethren were content.
### Response: Hong pai un, Ishmael mite kiangah amah zuak ni. Amah bangmah cih kei ni. Bang hang hiam cih leh amah i sanggampa, eima cilesa ahi hi,” a ci hi. A sanggamte in ama thu mang hi.
### Input: Translate to Zolai: Then there passed by Midianites merchantmen; and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver: and they brought Joseph into Egypt.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Reuben returned unto the pit; and, behold, Joseph was not in the pit; and he rent his clothes.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Judah saw there a daughter of a certain Canaanite, whose name was Shua; and he took her, and went in unto her.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And Onan knew that the seed should not be his; and it came to pass, when he went in unto his brother's wife, that he spilled it on the ground, lest that he should give seed to his brother.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: Then said Judah to Tamar his daughter in law, Remain a widow at thy father's house, till Shelah my son be grown: for he said, Lest peradventure he die also, as his brethren did. And Tamar went and dwelt in her father's house.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And she put her widow's garments off from her, and covered her with a veil, and wrapped herself, and sat in an open place, which is by the way to Timnah; for she saw that Shelah was grown, and she was not given unto him to wife.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: When Judah saw her, he thought her to be an harlot; because she had covered her face.

### Response: “Manlangin i pa kiangah va ciahto unla ama kiangah, ‘Na tapa Josef in hih bangin ci hi: Pasian in Egypt gam khempeuh’ topa-in kei hong bawl khin hi. Ka kiangah hong paisuk hen, zekai kei hen.

### Response: Goshen leitangah na teng ding a, nang le na tate’ tate le na tuuhonte le na bawnghonte le na neih khempeuh tawh, nang pen kei tawh a kinai-in na om ding hi.

### Response: Hong tung ding kialpi kum nga omlai ahih manin tua lai-ah nang kong vak ding hi. Tua hi kei le nang le na innkuanpihte le na neih khempeuh in zawnna na ngah ding uh hi, ci hi,’ va ci un.

### Input: Translate to Zolai: When that year was ended, they came unto him the second year, and said unto him, We will not hide it from my lord, how that our money is spent; my lord also hath our herds of cattle; there is not ought left in the sight of my lord, but our bodies, and our lands:

### Input: Translate to Zolai: And as for the people, he removed them to cities from one end of the borders of Egypt even to the other end thereof.

### Response: Siampite’ leitang bek amah in lei lo hi. Bang hang hiam cih leh siampite in Faro’ tung panin a kiciangtan zah an ngah uh a, amaute tungah Faro in a piak tawh amaute nungtazo uh ahih ciangin amaute in a leitang uh zuak lo uh hi.
